entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a multi-level architecture that retains state across users, sessions, and agents. It utilizes a token-efficient single-pass extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval to fuse semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and relationships from interactions. It enhances Hermes with over 30 MCP tools and a hybrid search system that combines vector retrieval with graph-based ranking.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a continuous learning loop for agents through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It simplifies memory management with a two-line LLM wrapper and ensures privacy via per-user memory isolation using custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that tracks the evolving context of users, agents, and groups over time. It provides natural language querying for insight extraction and supports both Python and TypeScript SDKs with OpenAI-compatible context formatting.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses an iterative self-improvement loop to help agents master complex tasks through automated strategy evaluation. It generates structured playbooks and traces for long-term knowledge persistence with native integration for Hermes Agent.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides lossless context management for Hermes using a hierarchical DAG structure to prevent message loss during compaction. It utilizes a SQLite store for raw history and provides specialized tools for agents to search and expand compacted context nodes.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent by providing a self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory library. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval method involving vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal to share memory across different agent runtimes.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on efficiency, ContextPilot optimizes prefix reuse and deduplication to reduce prompt tokens by up to 36% in agentic workloads. It accelerates inference across various backends like vLLM and SGLang while preserving accuracy for long-context tasks.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format for transparent, local storage in multi-agent systems. It automates memory injection for Hermes and other coding agents using a zero-cost hybrid search approach.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases and media with automatic summarization and OCR capabilities. It uses edge tags to create bidirectional relationships between notes, exposing these insights to Hermes via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a high-performance, zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond local read and search operations. It leverages a SQLite backend with native vector support to ensure 100% local data privacy with no telemetry.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It uses a local-first architecture with SQLite-vec and provides an MCP-native server for seamless configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It is designed to unify skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a multi-tier word replacement table and a two-pass audit system to ensure agent-generated text remains undetectable and natural.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It is fully compatible with agentskills.io, allowing Hermes to quickly adopt professional frameworks for high-level consulting tasks.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata, significantly reducing token usage during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions using specialized presets for machine learning and architecture. It features automated self-checking loops to refine diagrams and supports animated connectors.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library equips Hermes with standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's decentralized oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes a local A/B testing framework to evaluate and improve the accuracy of blockchain-related agent responses.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill enables Hermes to weave prose, diagrams, and source code into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system via markdown-based directories, allowing Hermes instances to remember past work across sessions. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to train replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using both USD and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. They enable Hermes to handle precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to programmatically expand its own capabilities based on user interactions.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages structured memory and cross-feature learning through missions and milestones. It persists state locally and generates markdown briefs to facilitate seamless handoffs between different models.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking and autonomous decision-making. It includes built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery if a long-running task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This Model Context Protocol (MCP) client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token consumption during tool discovery. Its daemon architecture maintains persistent server connections and supports hot-reloading for dynamic skill updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-forge converts code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It provides a verifiable audit trail with line-level provenance to ensure the accuracy of the generated agent capabilities.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: Hermeshub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing Hermes skills with one-click simplicity. It prioritizes safety through automated security scanning and features an agent-to-agent feedback system to track reliability.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project provides a C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with integrated MCP support. It allows .NET developers to distribute skills as resources while utilizing progressive disclosure to manage metadata efficiently.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud instances to manage files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup script for secure HTTPS communication using Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring developers always have access to the latest builds. It features a unified proxy API for cross-provider compatibility and supports multi-agent orchestrators to manage complex, parallel workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that blocks unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes using pre-configured security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible setup that supports both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex dependencies, offering a portable GUI-based environment with a dynamic tool maker for custom automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for managing multiple agent instances and workspaces through a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling with integrated support for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this repository configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration layer that uses SQLite to manage multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph without external dependencies. It provides centralized task dispatching, spend monitoring, and a human-in-the-loop Aegis review system for task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms featuring durable memory, scheduled tasks, and support for over 23 LLM providers. It distinguishes itself through visual orchestration, allowing users to manage delegation workflows via organizational charts.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A specialized meta-optimization tool designed to improve harness code through an automated outer-loop search process. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track performance frontiers via structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars using Paperclip and Hermes Agent. The platform features a real-time dashboard to visualize agent tasks, code execution, and competitive scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A high-density coordination pipeline that manages 17 specialized agents across research, implementation, and infrastructure domains. It ensures reliability by enforcing mandatory quality gates through dedicated reviewer and tester agents.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native software development lifecycle (SDLC) that executes an autonomous eight-phase process from planning to health checks. It leverages the Supermodel MCP to provide structural code graphs, using graph-diff phase gates to enforce architectural quality.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: A delegation-focused framework that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code using an ACP-style approach. It maintains clean parent contexts through isolated workflows and enforces safety via configurable timeouts and character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI for monitoring token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents, including Hermes. It features a unique 3D contribution graph to visualize consumption patterns across different models and platforms.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based themes, ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk aesthetics. It supports partial skin definitions and default theme inheritance, enabling granular control over colors, ASCII banners, and UI labels.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, allowing it to run natively without external processes or OAuth. It specifically integrates MLOps capabilities for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training within the CCC environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), offering a human-visible interface for auditing agent actions. It automates page schema inference to help Hermes and other agents extract structured content from web environments more reliably.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework introduces per-folder scoping for Hermes, allowing developers to create localized agents with project-specific memories, tools, and instructions. It enables seamless switching between global and scoped agents to ensure the model remains grounded in the current workspace context.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that validates agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs or latency. It uses regex and heuristics to detect seven specific structural failure modes, ensuring YAML and JSON schemas align with the intended prompt logic.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and workspace files to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, and local credentials to maintain continuity across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent alongside LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a complete local-first environment. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and optimized routing for free models and local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-friendly web format. It features a three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files and view inline previews alongside their chat sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, offering unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It includes real-time session persistence, tool call expansion, and detailed usage analytics for tracking token costs across different platforms.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane supporting role-based dispatch and swarm orchestration. It integrates a Monaco-based file manager and a dedicated memory browser, allowing users to search and edit agent context directly within the interface.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing individual agent profiles with the ability to start, stop, and configure them via a browser-based terminal. It features an integrated xterm.js terminal with full PTY support and detailed token analytics for monitoring model usage and system metrics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS workspace utilizes direct SSH connections to ensure the remote host remains the single source of truth for all agent operations. The interface combines an embedded tabbed terminal with specialized views for Kanban boards, session data, and skill management.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed as a Telegram Mini App that supports spawning up to five concurrent agent instances. It features a terminal-style chat with slash commands and real-time system monitoring gauges for CPU, memory, and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no administrative rights and comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for local TTS, music, and ComfyUI generation. It includes a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime and offers granular permission controls for local model execution.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment configuration. It provides a unified GUI for managing 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, featuring full-text search across all integrated session histories.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed for tracking session costs and agent processes. It allows for read-only configuration viewing and management of scheduled cron jobs, supported by FTS5 search capabilities for conversation history inspection.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that utilizes SSE streaming and visual timelines for tool execution. It features an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, alongside profile-scoped management for global memory and agent configurations.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic React application features a single-file architecture that streams logs and chat responses in real time. It uniquely includes an integrated Kanban board for automatic task detection and tracking based on agent interactions within the workspace.
